roundingContractDisclosure.summary: All dollar amounts are displayed to the cent; actual amounts are precise to the cent, and projected values are rounded to the nearest dollar before display. Rates and coverture fractions are shown as a percentage to two decimals.

entries.fullCite: AAML Considerations (2007): 30% payor − 20% payee gross, 40% combined-income cap
entries.description: Benchmark spousal-support estimate: the LESSER of (a) 30% of payor gross income minus 20% of payee gross income and (b) 40% of combined gross income minus payee gross income, floored at zero.
Advisory spousal-support duration band keyed to marriage length.
Maryland authority recognizing consideration of the AAML benchmark formula.
Maryland child-support guideline framework and schedule.
Maryland statutory alimony factors.

Maryland authority on above-schedule child support: where combined income exceeds the top of the statutory guideline schedule, the award is committed to the court’s discretion; ClearPath uses the top-of-schedule basic obligation as a conservative computational baseline, then apportions by income share.

Marital share = total present value × the coverture fraction. The fraction is marital-service months (from the later of date-of-hire and date-of-marriage to the earlier of the marital cutoff date and the retirement date) ÷ total-service months (date-of-hire to retirement date), counting each whole or partial calendar month as one (a trailing partial month counts as a full month unless the period ends on the first of the month).
Coverture (time-rule) marital-share authority.

entries.fullCite: Mosley v. Mosley, 19 Va. App. 192, 450 S.E.2d 161 (1994)
entries.description: Coverture (time-rule) marital-share authority.
Coverture (time-rule) marital-share authority for a defined-benefit pension.

entries.fullCite: In re Marriage of Hug, 154 Cal. App. 3d 780 (1984)
entries.description: Deferred-comp time rule, measured in DAYS (distinct from the pension coverture month-count): marital fraction = days from date of HIRE to the earlier of separation and the tranche vesting date ÷ days from date of hire to the tranche vesting date (clamped 0–1); marital shares = round(granted shares × fraction). California authority applied as a valuation METHOD; not a statement that California law governs this matter.

entries.fullCite: In re Marriage of Nelson, 177 Cal. App. 3d 150 (1986)
entries.description: Deferred-comp time rule, measured in DAYS: marital fraction = days from the GRANT date to the earlier of separation and the tranche vesting date ÷ days from the grant date to the tranche vesting date (clamped 0–1); marital shares = round(granted shares × fraction). California authority applied as a valuation METHOD; not a statement that California law governs this matter.

Present value of an accrued defined-benefit pension: PV = monthly benefit × 12 × an annuity-due factor × the deferral discount to the valuation date. The annuity-due factor is the sum, over each future year the participant survives, of the survival probability ÷ (1 + segment-2 rate)^year, using the § 417(e)(3) segment-rate structure and the applicable mortality table (survival probabilities); the deferral discount is (1 + segment-2 rate)^(−years to assumed retirement).

Capital-gain exclusion on the sale of a principal residence: up to $250,000 of gain (single) or $500,000 (married filing jointly) is excluded; where the gain is below the applicable cap it is fully excluded, leaving zero taxable gain and therefore zero estimated tax.
Spousal ownership/use tacking for the principal-residence gain exclusion.
Carryover basis for property transferred incident to divorce.
Year-end (Dec. 31) marital-status determination for filing status.
Child Tax Credit applied in the filing-status comparison.

entries.shortCite: Point-in-time tax discount (Sutherland "Point in Time" formula)
entries.fullCite: Sutherland, "Is Your Retirement Plan Tax Discount Too High?", DFA Journal (IDFA), Q2 2025 — point-in-time 401(k) tax-discount formula; ClearPath disclosed implementation, formula in Appendix A.
entries.description: Point-in-time tax discount on a tax-deferred balance: discount rate = TR ÷ [((1 + i)^n − 1)(1 − TR) + 1], where TR is the effective tax rate, i the annual discount rate, and n the years to the withdrawal midpoint; the dollar discount is the account balance times this rate.
Federal income-tax brackets and standard deductions for the stated tax year.
Disclosed v1 simplification: a flat 15% long-term capital-gains rate (not the full statutory rate schedule).

entries.fullCite: Social Security Administration contribution and benefit base (year-pinned)
entries.description: Social Security contribution and benefit base for the stated year (Social Security tax cap).

entries.fullCite: IRS notice, 401(k) elective deferral + catch-up limits (year-pinned)
entries.description: 401(k) elective-deferral and catch-up limits for the stated year.
Private-mortgage-insurance cancellation thresholds (78% scheduled / 80% requested loan-to-value).
